meta.name: Beef Vegetable Soup
meta.subtitle: Hearty, freezer-friendly, batch-cooking baseline • “use what you have” vegetables

source.notes: Designed for SSA batch cooking/freezer logic. Supports stew beef or ground beef, mixed vegetables, and optional barley/rice. Includes make-ahead and scaling notes.

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on simmer and add-ins.

tasteProfile.notes: Savory beef + vegetables in a lightly tomatoed broth; customizable with herbs, spice, and starch add-ins.

ingredients.label: Beef stew meat (chuck) cut into 1-inch pieces (or ground beef—see variation)
ingredients.notes: About 2 lb. Chuck or round works well. Trim large hard fat.

ingredients.label: Salt (for seasoning beef)
ingredients.notes: About 1 teaspoon; adjust later based on broth.

ingredients.label: Beef broth/stock (low-sodium preferred) or chicken broth
ingredients.notes: About 7–8 cups. Water can replace up to half if using better-than-bouillon later.

ingredients.label: Water (optional, for volume/adjusting thickness)
ingredients.notes: Add if you want a brothier soup or if simmer reduces too far.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, diced (1/2-inch)
ingredients.notes: About 3 medium Yukon gold or russet.

ingredients.label: Green beans (fresh or frozen), cut
ingredients.notes: Add near the end if tender; earlier if using very firm.

ingredients.label: Mixed vegetables (frozen) as a swap option
ingredients.notes: Use instead of some individual veg if needed.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar or red wine vinegar (optional, brightens)
Pearled barley (optional add-in; cook in soup)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Adds body; increases cook time slightly.

ingredients.label: Cooked rice (optional add-in; add to bowl)
ingredients.notes: Add to bowls when serving for best freezer/leftovers texture.

steps.title: Season beef and sear
instructions: Pat beef dry and season with the salt and pepper listed for beef.
Heat oil in a large pot/Dutch oven over medium-high.
Sear beef in batches until browned on 2–3 sides (don’t crowd the pot). Transfer browned beef to a plate.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Reduce heat to medium. Add onion, carrots, and celery to the pot (add a splash of broth if the pot is dry).
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Tomato base and deglaze
instructions: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute to deepen flavor.
Add diced tomatoes with juices and scrape the pot bottom to loosen browned bits.

steps.title: Simmer until beef is tender
instructions: Return seared beef (and any juices) to the pot.
Add broth, bay leaf, thyme, and optional Italian seasoning. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover partially and simmer 45–60 minutes until beef is starting to get tender.

steps.title: Add potatoes and longer-cooking vegetables
instructions: Add diced potatoes (and barley if using). Simmer 15–20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
If using green beans that are very firm, add them here as well.

steps.title: Add quick vegetables, adjust seasoning, and finish
instructions: Add optional corn, peas, and/or mixed vegetables. Simmer 3–7 minutes until heated through and tender.
Stir in Worcestershire (optional). Taste and adjust salt/pepper.
Turn off heat. Add vinegar (optional) and parsley (optional). Remove bay leaf. Serve.

variations.name: Ground beef version (fast)
variations.change: Use 680g ground beef. Brown it (Step 1) and drain excess fat, then proceed. Simmer time can drop to ~25–35 minutes total.

variations.name: Freezer-optimized (best texture)
variations.change: Freeze soup without potatoes (potatoes can get grainy). Replace potatoes with barley, or add fresh potatoes when reheating.
variations.notes: You can also portion cooked rice into bowls and ladle hot soup over it.

variations.name: Spicy version
variations.change: Add 1–2g crushed red pepper or a diced jalapeño with the aromatics.
variations.notes: Finish with a splash of hot sauce if desired.

variations.name: Cabbage-forward
variations.change: Add 200–300g shredded cabbage during the last 10–12 minutes of simmering.

bestPractices: Cool quickly (shallow pans), then portion into labeled containers.
Freeze flat in bags for space efficiency.
For best texture, add starches (rice/pasta) at serving time.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Flavor improves by day 2.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months. Freeze without potatoes for best texture (optional).
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer; add water/broth if thickened.

notes: If broth tastes flat: add a small splash of vinegar, a pinch more salt, and/or Worcestershire.
If soup is too thick: add water or broth and simmer 5 minutes.
Searing beef adds depth; if skipping sear, increase simmer time slightly and consider Worcestershire.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions. Optimized for batch/freezer workflows.
